req.title: Device data ingest (audit)
req.statement: A registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest; unregistered devices shall be rejected. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-097 mandates three orthogonal concerns: (1) registered devices can ingest FHIR Observations and have them stored with normalization, (2) unregistered/invalid-cert devices receive 401 and are rejected, and (3) an AuditEvent is emitted for every ingest operation regardless of outcome. Test selection uses EP to partition registered vs unregistered device space, NEG for the invalid-certificate rejection path, BVA for the minimal-valid single-Observation payload boundary, STC for the state transition from device-registered to observation-stored with audit emitted, and DT to cover the decision table row where cert is valid AND device is registered AND payload is well-formed. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that register/deregister devices and roll back state, making them idempotent.

tests.name: test_registered_device_ingest_stores_observation_and_emits_audit
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable at FHIR_BASE_URL
Device 'dev-001' is registered in the device registry with a valid TLS certificate
No prior Observation with identifier 'obs-ep-001' exists
actions: POST a well-formed FHIR Observation resource to /Ingest using the registered device certificate
expected: POST /Ingest returns HTTP 201
Response body contains a FHIR Observation with id set by the server
GET /Observation?identifier=obs-ep-001 returns at least one resource
The stored Observation has normalization applied (valueQuantity unit is canonical)
GET /AuditEvent?entity=obs-ep-001 returns at least one AuditEvent with outcome='0'
trace: R-HLT-097: registered device pushes Observations via FHIR ingest
R-HLT-097: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_registered_device_ingest_stores_observation_and_emits_audit(
    registered_device_session,  # <fixture>: requests.Session with valid cert
    fhir_base_url,              # <fixture>: str base URL
    cleanup_observation,        # <fixture>: deletes obs by identifier on teardown
):
    identifier = "obs-ep-001"
    cleanup_observation(identifier)

    payload = <build_observation_payload>(identifier, value=98.6, unit="degF")

    resp = registered_device_session.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Ingest",
        json=payload,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"

    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Observation"
    assert body.get("id"), "Server must assign an id"

    search = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation",
        params={"identifier": identifier},
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert search.status_code == 200
    bundle = search.json()
    assert bundle.get("total", 0) >= 1, "Observation must be stored"

    stored = bundle["entry"][0]["resource"]
    unit = stored["valueQuantity"]["code"]
    assert unit == "Cel", f"Normalization expected Cel, got {unit}"

    audit = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": identifier},
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert audit.status_code == 200
    events = audit.json()
    assert events.get("total", 0) >= 1, "AuditEvent must be emitted"
    assert events["entry"][0]["resource"]["outcome"] == "0"

tests.name: test_unregistered_device_ingest_returns_401_and_emits_audit
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable at FHIR_BASE_URL
Device 'dev-unregistered' holds an invalid or self-signed certificate not trusted by fhir-lite
Device 'dev-unregistered' is NOT present in the device registry
actions: POST a well-formed FHIR Observation resource to /Ingest using the unregistered device certificate
Query /AuditEvent for the rejected request to verify audit emission
expected: POST /Ingest returns HTTP 401
Response body contains an OperationOutcome with issue.severity='error'
No Observation is created for identifier 'obs-neg-001'
GET /AuditEvent for the rejected request returns at least one AuditEvent with outcome != '0'
trace: R-HLT-097: unregistered devices shall be rejected
R-HLT-097: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unregistered_device_ingest_returns_401_and_emits_audit(
    unregistered_device_session,  # <fixture>: requests.Session with invalid cert
    fhir_base_url,                # <fixture>: str base URL
):
    identifier = "obs-neg-001"
    payload = <build_observation_payload>(identifier, value=37.0, unit="Cel")

    resp = unregistered_device_session.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Ingest",
        json=payload,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"

    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(i["severity"] == "error" for i in issues)

    search = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation",
        params={"identifier": identifier},
        timeout=10,
    )
    bundle = search.json()
    assert bundle.get("total", 0) == 0, "No Observation must be stored for rejected device"

    audit = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": identifier},
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert audit.status_code == 200
    events = audit.json()
    assert events.get("total", 0) >= 1, "AuditEvent must be emitted even for rejected requests"
    outcomes = [e["resource"]["outcome"] for e in events["entry"]]
    assert any(o != "0" for o in outcomes), "AuditEvent outcome must indicate failure"

tests.name: test_minimal_single_observation_boundary_payload_accepted
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable at FHIR_BASE_URL
Device 'dev-001' is registered with a valid TLS certificate
Payload contains exactly the minimum required FHIR Observation fields (resourceType, status, code, subject)
actions: POST the minimum-field FHIR Observation payload to /Ingest using the registered device certificate
expected: POST /Ingest returns HTTP 201
Server accepts the minimal payload without rejecting for missing optional fields
GET /Observation?identifier=obs-bva-001 returns the stored resource
GET /AuditEvent?entity=obs-bva-001 returns at least one AuditEvent with outcome='0'
trace: R-HLT-097: registered device pushes Observations via FHIR ingest
R-HLT-097: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
BVA lower boundary: exactly the minimum fields required by FHIR Observation profile
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_minimal_single_observation_boundary_payload_accepted(
    registered_device_session,  # <fixture>: requests.Session with valid cert
    fhir_base_url,              # <fixture>: str base URL
    cleanup_observation,        # <fixture>: deletes obs by identifier on teardown
):
    identifier = "obs-bva-001"
    cleanup_observation(identifier)

    minimal_payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8310-5"}]
        },
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/pat-001"},
        "identifier": [{"value": identifier}]
    }

    resp = registered_device_session.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Ingest",
        json=minimal_payload,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    assert resp.json().get("resourceType") == "Observation"

    search = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation",
        params={"identifier": identifier},
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert search.status_code == 200
    assert search.json().get("total", 0) >= 1, "Minimal Observation must be stored"

    audit = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": identifier},
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert audit.status_code == 200
    events = audit.json()
    assert events.get("total", 0) >= 1, "AuditEvent must be emitted for minimal payload"
    assert events["entry"][0]["resource"]["outcome"] == "0"

tests.name: test_device_state_transition_registered_to_observation_stored_with_audit
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable at FHIR_BASE_URL
A device registration API is available at /Device
actions: Register device 'dev-stc-001' via POST /Device (transition: UNREGISTERED -> REGISTERED)
POST a FHIR Observation to /Ingest with 'dev-stc-001' credentials (transition: REGISTERED -> OBSERVATION_STORED)
Verify Observation is stored and AuditEvent emitted
expected: POST /Device returns 201 confirming registration
POST /Ingest returns 201 confirming Observation stored
GET /Observation?identifier=obs-stc-001 returns the resource
GET /AuditEvent?entity=obs-stc-001 returns AuditEvent with outcome='0'
DELETE /Device/dev-stc-001 returns 200 or 204 confirming cleanup
trace: R-HLT-097: registered device pushes Observations via FHIR ingest
R-HLT-097: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
STC: covers UNREGISTERED -> REGISTERED -> OBSERVATION_STORED state path
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_device_state_transition_registered_to_observation_stored_with_audit(
    fhir_base_url,           # <fixture>: str base URL
    admin_session,           # <fixture>: requests.Session with admin privileges
    device_cert_for,         # <fixture>: fn(device_id) -> (cert, key) tuple
):
    device_id = "dev-stc-001"
    identifier = "obs-stc-001"

    reg_resp = admin_session.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Device",
        json=<build_device_payload>(device_id),
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert reg_resp.status_code == 201, f"Registration failed: {reg_resp.text}"

    try:
        cert, key = device_cert_for(device_id)
        session = <make_mtls_session>(cert, key)
        payload = <build_observation_payload>(identifier, value=36.6, unit="Cel")

        ingest_resp = session.post(
            f"{fhir_base_url}/Ingest",
            json=payload,
            timeout=10,
        )
        assert ingest_resp.status_code == 201, f"Ingest failed: {ingest_resp.text}"

        search = requests.get(
            f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation",
            params={"identifier": identifier}, timeout=10,
        )
        assert search.json().get("total", 0) >= 1

        audit = requests.get(
            f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent",
            params={"entity": identifier}, timeout=10,
        )
        assert audit.json().get("total", 0) >= 1
        assert audit.json()["entry"][0]["resource"]["outcome"] == "0"
    finally:
        admin_session.delete(f"{fhir_base_url}/Device/{device_id}", timeout=10)

tests.name: test_decision_table_valid_cert_registered_device_well_formed_payload
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable at FHIR_BASE_URL
Device 'dev-001' is registered with a valid TLS certificate
Observation payload is well-formed and includes all mandatory FHIR fields
Device certificate is valid (not expired, correct CA chain)
actions: POST a complete FHIR Observation (all decision table conditions TRUE) to /Ingest
expected: POST /Ingest returns HTTP 201 (DT row: cert=valid, registered=true, payload=valid -> accept)
Observation is persisted and retrievable
Normalization is applied to valueQuantity
AuditEvent emitted with outcome='0' and action='C' (create)
trace: R-HLT-097: registered device pushes Observations via FHIR ingest
R-HLT-097: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
DT row: cert_valid=T, device_registered=T, payload_valid=T -> HTTP 201 + stored + audit success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_decision_table_valid_cert_registered_device_well_formed_payload(
    registered_device_session,  # <fixture>: requests.Session with valid cert
    fhir_base_url,              # <fixture>: str base URL
    cleanup_observation,        # <fixture>: deletes obs by identifier on teardown
):
    identifier = "obs-dt-001"
    cleanup_observation(identifier)

    payload = <build_full_observation_payload>(
        identifier=identifier,
        value=37.2,
        unit="degF",
        device_ref="Device/dev-001",
        category="vital-signs",
    )

    resp = registered_device_session.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Ingest",
        json=payload,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"DT all-true row must yield 201: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "Observation"
    assert body.get("id"), "Server id must be present"

    search = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation",
        params={"identifier": identifier}, timeout=10,
    )
    assert search.json().get("total", 0) >= 1
    stored = search.json()["entry"][0]["resource"]
    assert stored["valueQuantity"]["code"] == "Cel", "Normalization to SI unit required"

    audit = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": identifier}, timeout=10,
    )
    events = audit.json()
    assert events.get("total", 0) >= 1
    event = events["entry"][0]["resource"]
    assert event["outcome"] == "0"
    assert event["action"] == "C", "AuditEvent action must be Create"
